_comment: v1.49.568 baseline fixture for default-off invariance tests (DRIFT-27). Each entry has a drift-layer input and the expected output when ALL drift flags are false (default). Default-off MUST produce output byte-identical to input across all seven defense modules.

flag_off_pairs.description: Short factual claim — knowledge drift layer must pass through unchanged.
flag_off_pairs.input: Marie Curie was born in Warsaw in 1867.
flag_off_pairs.expected_output: Marie Curie was born in Warsaw in 1867.

flag_off_pairs.description: Multi-sentence biography — passes through unchanged under flag-off.
flag_off_pairs.input: Alan Turing was born in London in 1912. He was a mathematician and computer scientist. He developed the concept of the universal computing machine.
flag_off_pairs.expected_output: Alan Turing was born in London in 1912. He was a mathematician and computer scientist. He developed the concept of the universal computing machine.

flag_off_pairs.description: High-drift content — still passes through unchanged under flag-off.
flag_off_pairs.input: Isaac Newton was born in 1643 in Woolsthorpe. He discovered the law of gravitation. Zephyr crystalized quinoa fungus amid volcanic archipelago precipitation event.
flag_off_pairs.expected_output: Isaac Newton was born in 1643 in Woolsthorpe. He discovered the law of gravitation. Zephyr crystalized quinoa fungus amid volcanic archipelago precipitation event.

flag_off_pairs.description: Alignment-surface activation snapshot input — task-drift monitor is a no-op under flag-off.
Retrieval context — stale-index and grounding checks are no-ops under flag-off.
flag_off_pairs.input: The capital of France is Paris. Napoleon was a French military leader. The Eiffel Tower was built in 1889.
flag_off_pairs.expected_output: The capital of France is Paris. Napoleon was a French military leader. The Eiffel Tower was built in 1889.
